name: General IPA Feature Inventory
source: Compiled from Chomsky & Halle (1968), Hayes (2009), Clements & Hume (1995), Ladefoged & Maddieson (1996), and post-2000 research. Cross-system verification v2.1: corrections applied per Miller (2011/2016) on click posterior closure, Walker (2011) on laryngeal sonorance, and Hayes (2009) on strident/coronal. See accompanying document 'Distinctive Features v2' and inline notes.

notes.consonantal: ɹ ɻ ʋ are [+consonantal] per cluster/sonority/syllabic evidence. ɰ ɥ are [-consonantal] (true glides). ʔ is [-consonantal, -sonorant]: cross-system majority (SPE, Hayes, PHOIBLE, Clements & Hume). It lacks supralaryngeal constriction. [+consonantal] for ʔ is a minority language-specific position only.
notes.strident: ɬ ɮ are [-strident]. Navajo sibilant harmony shows lateral fricatives are fully transparent — neither trigger nor target — proving they are not sibilants. p͡f b͡v are also [-strident] following Hayes (2009), which restricts strident to coronals.
notes.implosives: Default [-sonorant] for ɓ ɗ ɠ ʛ. Sande & Oakley (2023) show 38% of languages have implosives pattern with sonorants; language-specific evidence should override.
notes.glottal_stop: ʔ is [-consonantal, -sonorant, +constrgl] — cross-system consensus (SPE, Hayes, PHOIBLE, Clements & Hume). It lacks supralaryngeal constriction, hence [-consonantal]. In the Blevins analysis ʔ would additionally be [+sonorant]; in some language-specific analyses it is treated as [+consonantal] when it patterns with oral stops, but this is not the default.
notes.h: h is [-consonantal, +sonorant] (placeless voiceless glide). Harmony transparency data supports [+sonorant] (Walker 2011). PHOIBLE codes h as [-sonorant]; this inventory follows Hayes/SPE.
notes.clicks: Click posterior closure is uvular [DORSAL, -high, +back] per Miller et al. (2009a) ultrasound data on N|uu confirmed by Miller (2016) on Mangetti Dune !Xung. Miller (2011): 'the posterior constriction is uvular in those Khoisan languages in which posterior place has been investigated with articulatory methods.' The term 'velaric airstream' reflects pre-ultrasound assumptions (Doke 1923). Laryngeal features (SpreadGl, Voice, Nasal) overlay clicks just as for pulmonic stops. ǃ anterior closure is [+anterior, -distributed] (alveolar default); postalveolar variants are [-anterior]. Only tenuis click base forms listed.
notes.trill_continuant: Trills and taps are [+continuant] following Hayes (2009). Steriade (1993) Aperture Theory provides compelling arguments for [-continuant] for trills in strong positions; see document for discussion.
notes.ATR: ATR is distinct from tense/lax per Tiede (1996) MRI evidence. [+ATR] applied to canonical [+ATR] vowels in African harmony systems (i e u o and tense counterparts). Needs language-specific calibration.
notes.approximant: [+approximant] marks the class of vowels + glides + liquids (all sonorants except nasals). Optional feature; many analyses express this class as [+sonorant, -nasal].
notes.rhotic_vowel: ɚ (rhotacised schwa) is a vowel with [+coronal] specification from the r-colouring gesture; [CORONAL, -anterior, +distributed] captures the tongue shape.
notes.ejectives: Ejective consonants (p' t' k' s' ʃ' t͡s') are [-voice, -spreadgl, +constrgl]. [+spreadgl, +constrgl] simultaneously is physically impossible. Ejective fricatives (s' ʃ') follow the same laryngeal pattern as ejective stops — SpreadGl corrected from + to - in v2.1.
notes.alveolopalatals: t͡ɕ d͡ʑ ɕ ʑ: [-anterior, +distributed, DORSAL+, +high, -back]. Majority analysis: postalveolar [-anterior] with palatal secondary articulation captured by DORSAL [+high, -back]. The dual CORONAL+DORSAL specification distinguishes them from plain postalveolars (ʃ ʒ t͡ʃ d͡ʒ). Anterior value corrected from + to - in v2.1 per Clements & Hume (1995) and PHOIBLE.
notes.labiodental_affricates: p͡f b͡v are [-strident]: Hayes (2009) restricts [strident] to coronals. Labiodental identity is captured by [LABIAL, +labiodental]. SPE gives [+strident] for labiodentals; this inventory follows Hayes. Strident corrected from + to - in v2.1.
notes.j_glide_coronal: j ɥ are [-CORONAL], following Hayes (2009), SPE, and PHOIBLE. Clements & Hume (1995) and Hume (1994) assign CORONAL to palatal glides and front vowels; Halle, Vaux & Wolfe (2000) explicitly reject this. [-CORONAL] is the cross-system majority. CORONAL removed from j and ɥ in v2.1.
notes.central_open_mid: ɜ ɞ are central vowels [-CORONAL, -back]. They are the open-mid counterparts of ɘ/ɵ, not front vowels. CORONAL removed in v2.1 — central vowels do not bear CORONAL in any major framework.
notes.open_a_central: a is [-CORONAL, -back] (phonologically central) following Hayes (2009). IPA classifies /a/ as 'open front', but phonological convention treats it as central — it is the unrounded counterpart of ɑ (+back) and pairs with ɑ on the [back] dimension. CORONAL removed in v2.1.
